name: IDOR via role_arn Parameter in /api/permission-check Allows Unauthenticated IAM Role Enumeration and Cross-Space Permission Probing
description: The GET `/api/permission-check` endpoint accepts an arbitrary `role_arn` query parameter and attempts IAM `ListAttachedRolePolicies` operations on the specified role without any authentication or authorization checks. This allows any unauthenticated user to: (1) enumerate IAM roles in AWS account 111111111111 via differential error responses (NoSuchEntity for non-existing roles vs `_fetch_tagged_resources` error for existing roles where policies were successfully retrieved vs AccessDenied for existing roles in different permission scopes), (2) probe permission data for roles belonging to different agent spaces constituting cross-tenant IDOR, and (3) discover the internal IAM identity of the dashboard service (`arn:aws:sts::111111111111:assumed-role/devops-agent-test-dashboard-role`). The `role_arn` parameter is a direct object reference that is never validated against any requester identity, ownership, or space membership. The application hosts multiple distinct agent spaces with different IAM roles, and the endpoint allows probing any of them without belonging to the respective space.
expected_root_cause: INSECURE_DIRECT_OBJECT_REFERENCE 취약점 — IDOR via role_arn Parameter in /api/permission-check Allows Unauthenticated IAM Role Enumeration and Cross-Space Permission Probing
